question: Why does Eddy collapse after losing the poker hand?
answer: Eddy collapses after losing the poker hand because he is overwhelmed by the pressure of owing Hatchet half a million dollars and the threat of severe consequences, including losing his father's bar and suffering physical harm if he fails to repay the debt by Sunday.
related_context: After losing the poker hand, Eddy sits frozen as Hatchet leans in and demands repayment of the half-million-dollar debt by Sunday. He warns that if the money isn't paid, Eddy will face increasingly severe consequences, including losing his father's bar and suffering physical harm. Eddy pushes himself up from the table, unsteady on his feet, and stumbles toward the door. He barely makes it outside before collapsing to his knees, vomiting onto the ground. His breath comes in ragged gasps as he slumps against the wall, drenched in sweat and trembling, unable to stand.
